body:
Hi Stan,

Following up on my Wake up order from last week — ticket #4821 if that helps narrow it down. I still haven't received a shipping confirmation, and my original ETA was Monday.

I keep a pretty structured morning schedule — up early, Peloton before the clinic shift — so I was planning around having this set up by now. It's Tuesday and I'm working off my old setup, which isn't ideal.

Can you confirm whether it shipped and, if there's been a delay, what the revised timeline looks like? No long explanation needed, just the facts.

Thanks,
Heather Cole
Fox Valley Women's Health Clinic, Ashwaubenon

Hi Heather,

Apologies for the radio silence — I pulled up ticket #4821. Your order did ship Monday morning, but there was a carrier scan error that blocked the confirmation email from sending. I've manually resent it to the address on file; you should see it within the hour.

Current tracking puts delivery tomorrow by end of day. I've added a priority notification flag to your account so you'll get a real-time update the moment there's movement.

Sorry this interrupted your routine. If it doesn't land by tomorrow evening, reply here and I'll escalate to our logistics team directly — no need to start a new ticket.

Best,
Stan Cole
Customer Support — Wake up

Hi Stan,

Following up on our earlier exchange about my morning supplement order. The Vitamin D3 2000 IU and omega-3 1200mg are routine refills, but I wanted to confirm you have the enteric-coated version of the omega-3 on file — I take it with breakfast and the non-coated softgels have made for some memorable Peloton rides. Not an experience I'm looking to repeat.

Also want to verify the D3 is softgel, not tablet. I see patients swap to the tablet form and then can't figure out why they're still deficient. I'd rather not troubleshoot my own absorption at 6am before a clinic shift.

Let me know what's showing on my account. Everything else should be a straightforward refill.

Thanks,
Heather Cole, NP

Hi Heather,

Good news on both counts — the omega-3 on file is the enteric-coated 1200mg softgels, and the D3 is the 2000 IU softgel formulation. Both are in stock.

Refill is processed and will be ready for pickup Thursday afternoon. If you'd prefer it routed to Fox Valley Women's Health Clinic in Ashwaubenon, I can arrange that instead — just let me know by Wednesday EOD.

One heads-up: the omega-3 supplier is switching packaging next month. Same formula, same dose, just a different bottle. Wanted to flag it so there's no confusion when you grab it.

Stan Cole
Patient Support

Stan,

Thursday pickup works fine — coordinating a delivery to the clinic on a busy day isn't worth the hassle.

Appreciate the packaging heads-up. I'd much rather know in advance than stand in the pharmacy second-guessing myself before a full patient schedule. I'll make a note.

All good on my end. Thanks for the quick turnaround.

Heather

Hi Stan,

Following up on ticket #PCL-884201 I opened last Thursday. The resistance calibration issue is still happening. I'm on this bike four mornings a week — 30-minute rides before I head into the clinic — and the resistance has been reading roughly 8–10 units high from warm-up through the end of class. I've done two hard power cycles and unplugged overnight. No change.

I need my mornings to actually function, so I'd rather not keep guessing at workarounds. Can you confirm whether this is a firmware issue or whether a tech visit is warranted? I'm in Ashwaubenon, WI if that affects scheduling.

Thanks,
Heather Cole

Hi Heather,

Thanks for following up — I pulled ticket #PCL-884201 and reviewed your notes. What you're describing is consistent with a calibration drift we've been seeing in bikes from the Q3 2022 production run. Good news: it's addressable without a tech visit in most cases.

Please try this sequence: hold the resistance knob down for 10 seconds while powering on, then navigate to Settings > Device Settings > Recalibrate Resistance and follow the on-screen prompts. The process takes about four minutes. If the values are still off on your next ride, reply here and I'll escalate to a field tech appointment at no charge under your warranty.

Appreciate your patience with this.

Stan Cole
Peloton Member Support

Hi Stan,

Ran through the recalibration sequence this morning before my ride. Resistance is reading normally now — warm-up felt right and the numbers tracked with where I'd expect them through the 30 minutes. Small miracle.

One thing I did notice: my output was noticeably lower than my usual Tuesday/Thursday numbers. Could be my legs were already tired from compensating against inflated resistance all last week, or I'm just slower on Mondays like a normal person. I'll give it a few rides before I worry about it.

Thanks for the clear instructions. If it drifts again I'll know to try this before filing a new ticket.

Heather

Hi Heather,

Glad the recalibration worked. The output dip is completely expected — when resistance reads artificially high, you unconsciously adjust effort upward to match perceived difficulty, so your muscles have essentially been training against a heavier load. Now that calibration is accurate, your output metrics will look lower until your pacing recalibrates over the next week or so. Not a fitness regression, just the numbers catching up to reality.

I'm marking #PCL-884201 resolved but leaving it open for 72 hours in case anything resurfaces. If the drift comes back, reference this ticket number so we can skip the intake steps.

Enjoy the rides.

Stan Cole
Peloton Member Support

Hi Stan,

Following up on the Thursday shift start adjustment I requested last week — I'd asked to push the 8:00 to 8:30 to deal with a scheduling conflict on my end. Didn't see anything updated in the portal yet and we're getting close to the week.

Fox Valley's usually on top of these, so I'm guessing it's just a lag in the system, but wanted to confirm before I lock in the rest of my schedule. Let me know if you still need something from me to make it official.

Thanks,
Heather Cole, NP
Fox Valley Women's Health Clinic — Ashwaubenon

Hi Heather,

You're all set — the 8:30 Thursday start is confirmed on our end. The scheduling portal typically runs 24–48 hours behind actual changes, so you should see it reflected by tomorrow at the latest. No further action needed from you.

If anything still looks off closer to Thursday, just shoot me a note and I'll pull it up directly. Appreciate you flagging it.

Best,
Stan Cole
Clinic Support — Fox Valley Women's Health

Hi Stan,

Following up on my ticket from last week regarding the Gmail API integration — I still haven't gotten the OAuth flow working reliably. The app is stuck in Testing mode, which means my tokens expire every seven days and I'm re-authenticating constantly. Between morning clinic and finishing charts by end of shift, that's friction I don't have bandwidth for.

Specifically: I've added myself as a test user in the Google Cloud Console, but the app keeps prompting for re-authorization at random intervals. Is there a way to move to production without the full verification review, or is there a cleaner fix I'm missing?

Thanks,
Heather Cole, NP
Fox Valley Women's Health Clinic

Hi Heather,

Thanks for the follow-up. The 7-day token expiry is expected for apps in Testing mode — it's Google's way of enforcing the verification process. There are two paths forward depending on your setup.

If you're on a Google Workspace account, you can set the OAuth app to 'Internal,' which limits access to your organization and bypasses the full public verification. Tokens stay valid on the standard refresh cycle. If you're on a personal Gmail account, you can add up to 100 test users directly in the consent screen settings — that won't fix the 7-day limit, but it does give you more control over who's affected.

The cleanest long-term solution is submitting for production verification. It takes 1-2 weeks and requires a privacy policy URL, but once approved, token expiry is no longer an issue. Can you confirm whether your account is Workspace or personal Gmail so I can point you to the right steps?

Best,
Stan Cole
Support Team

Stan,

Personal Gmail — Fox Valley runs Epic for everything clinical, so this is a side project I stitched together on my own time. Adding test users sounds like the lowest-lift option for now. I'll log in tonight after clinic and try that route.

If the 7-day limit stays a problem long-term I'll look at the production verification process, but I'd rather confirm the rest of the integration actually works before I start writing a privacy policy at 10 PM.

I'll follow up if I hit a wall. Thanks for the clear breakdown — genuinely useful.

Heather

Hi Stan,

Following up on ticket #8841 from last week — the Google Calendar API sync still isn't pulling my clinic blocks correctly into the shared staff calendar. Shifts at Fox Valley show as all-day events instead of the actual 7:30–4:30 window, which means my colleagues can't see when I'm actually available for consults.

I did re-authorize the OAuth scope as your colleague suggested, but no change. I've got a pretty packed Tuesday starting early and need this sorted before then — the workaround of manually entering everything is eating into the 20 minutes I actually have between charting and getting out the door.

Can you confirm whether this is a calendar settings issue on our end or something in how the API is reading the event duration field?

Thanks,
Heather Cole, NP
Fox Valley Women's Health Clinic

Hi Heather,

Thanks for the follow-up and sorry for the delay getting back to you. I pulled up ticket #8841 and reviewed the authorization logs — the OAuth re-auth went through cleanly, so that's not the culprit.

What I'm seeing is that the Google Calendar API is reading your clinic events as spanning midnight-to-midnight because the source calendar has the time zone offset misconfigured. Events created in Central Time are being ingested as UTC, which collapses them into all-day blocks on the receiving end. It's a known quirk when the organization's Google Workspace default time zone doesn't match the calendar owner's local setting.

Fix is straightforward: go to your Google Calendar settings → your Fox Valley calendar → scroll to Time Zone → make sure it's set to America/Chicago. Let me know once you've updated it and I'll re-trigger the sync from our side.

Best,
Stan Cole
Customer Support

Stan,

That did it. Changed the time zone to America/Chicago, you re-triggered the sync, and my Tuesday blocks are now showing up correctly — 7:30 to 4:30, right down to the lunch gap I block out around noon.

One follow-up question: I have a recurring 6:00 AM slot I block four mornings a week for personal time before my shift. It's not a clinic appointment but I do want it to show as busy on the shared calendar so nobody tries to schedule a team call then. Right now it's not syncing over at all — I'm guessing because it lives on my personal Google calendar rather than the Fox Valley one?

If that's the case I can just duplicate the block onto the clinic calendar. Just want to confirm before I do it manually four times a week indefinitely.

Thanks for the quick fix on the time zone.
Heather

Heather,

You've got it exactly right. The API integration is scoped to your Fox Valley Women's Health calendar only — personal calendar events won't sync across unless we expand the OAuth scope to include it, which typically requires your IT admin to approve for the whole organization. Probably more process than it's worth for a personal block.

Easiest path: create a recurring "Busy" event directly on the Fox Valley calendar for those four mornings. Takes about two minutes to set up as a weekly recurrence and it'll sync automatically going forward with no further action on your end.

I'm going to mark ticket #8841 resolved on our end, but reply here if anything else comes up — I'll keep the thread open for 30 days. Glad the time zone fix worked cleanly.

Best,
Stan Cole
Customer Support

Hi Stan,

Following up on our ticket from last week regarding the Google Drive API integration. I still can't get the service account to authenticate against our shared clinic drive — it's throwing a 403 on every request even after we updated the OAuth scopes. I've verified the credentials JSON is correct and the service account has editor access on the folder level.

I'm trying to automate document routing for Fox Valley Women's Health — we generate a fair amount of referral paperwork and having it land in the right shared folder without manual drag-and-drop would save real time between patients. Right now I'm testing locally before we hand this off to IT, so I'd rather not loop them in until I know the config is sound.

Can you take another look at the setup? Happy to share the sanitized config if that helps narrow it down.

Heather Cole, NP
Fox Valley Women's Health Clinic, Ashwaubenon

Hi Heather,

Thanks for the follow-up — the 403 despite correct scopes is almost always a domain delegation issue rather than a permissions problem at the folder level. For Workspace-managed accounts, the service account needs domain-wide delegation enabled in the Google Admin console, and the client ID has to be explicitly authorized for the scopes you're using. Without that step, folder-level editor access won't matter.

Here's what to check: in Google Admin go to Security > API Controls > Domain-wide Delegation, add your service account's client ID, and include https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive in the scope list. Then in your code make sure you're impersonating a real Workspace user (subject parameter in the credentials) rather than authenticating as the service account directly.

If you can share the sanitized config and the exact error response body, I can confirm whether that's the only blocker. Should be a quick fix once delegation is in place.

Best,
Stan Cole
Technical Support

Hi Stan,

Following up on the ticket I opened last week regarding the Spotify API authorization issue. The problem is still happening — when I try to connect my Spotify account through the Peloton app, I get a 403 error midway through the OAuth flow. It's been cutting out mid-ride, which is not ideal when you're 20 minutes into a class at 5:45am.

I've already tried revoking and re-granting app permissions, cleared cache, reinstalled the Peloton app twice. No change. My Spotify Premium subscription is active and I can confirm the account itself works fine standalone.

Can you check whether there's something on the API scope level blocking the connection? I'd rather have a concrete answer than another round of generic troubleshooting steps.

Thanks,
Heather Cole

Hi Heather,

Thanks for the follow-up and for being thorough with your troubleshooting steps — that actually saves us time.

I pulled up your account and I can see the authorization request is failing at the `user-modify-playback-state` scope. It looks like there was a permissions policy update pushed on June 18th that affected a subset of third-party integrations, and Peloton's API key falls in that bucket. This is on our end, not yours.

Our engineering team has a fix staged — estimated rollout is July 3rd. In the meantime, I can flag your account for a workaround that restores playback control without the full scope grant. Takes about 10 minutes on our side. Want me to go ahead and apply that?

Sorry for the disruption to your mornings.

Best,
Stan Cole | Spotify Developer Support

Stan,

Yes, please go ahead and apply the workaround. Appreciate you actually diagnosing the root cause instead of telling me to uninstall and reinstall a third time.

Good to know the fix is coming July 3rd — I can work around it until then as long as the workaround holds. If it breaks again before the patch rolls out, I'll reply to this thread directly.

Thanks for the quick turnaround.

Heather
